the next thing to be consider in this rule , be faith , out of the which the action must proceed . faith be take diverse in scripture , but that whereby we and our work be acceptable , be of two kind . the one concern the justification of our person ; the other the righteousness of our action . that which make our person acceptable , be our confidence in God , through Jesus Christ , whereby we be assure of pardon and grace . this which concern the righteousness of our action , be a assurance in our mind , that the thing which we be to do , be not against the law of God , as be set down in the five verse of this chapter . this last , be the faith which be require , the other be suppose : for the rule be give to Christians , who be justify by that faith , without which it be impossible to please God . Heb. 11. 6. now as the faith which concern the acceptation of our person , lean to the promise of the gospel ; so the faith that concern the righteousness of our action be ground on the precept of the law , in such thing as be command or forbid : but as for thing indifferent , that be neither command nor forbid , there be three general , whereon our faith must repose in the worship of God , piety , charity , and decency . first , we must know assure , that the thing which we be to do , tend to the glory of God ; at least , be no way derogative thereto : next , that it be not offensive to our neighbour , that be , it give he no just cause , nor occasion to scar , or take exception against our profession . Theise two ground we have in this chapter , the last we have in the end of the 14. chapter of the 1. corinth : let all thing be do grave and orderly . so whatsoever thing indifferent be not do with assurance that it be neither offensive to God , nor to our neighbour , nor undecent for our profession , it be sin . now what sin this be , which be last to be consider , be easy declare : for if it be offensive to God , or unseemly for our profession , whereof God be the author , it be sin against the first great commandment , thou shall love the lord thy god with all thy mind , heart and strength . and if it be offensive to thy neighbour , it be against the second , thou shall love thy neighbour , as thy self . if these ground be sure , it be manifest , that although indifferent thing be neither command , nor forbid particular in the word , yet the use and practice of they be subject to the general rule of piety and charity : and as every man in his private action , must have respect to these rule , Gal. 5. 13. and not abuse his liberty to licentiousness : so must the magistrate in the commonwealth set down order and law to be keep by subject , touch the use of such thing ; as of time and place , for justice , for market , for the exercise of arm , for use and abstinence from meat at certain time and season , which thing in themselves may be do indifferent at any time , or in any place , if policy and decency do not crave order to be keep , whereby the privilege of subject be not impair , but civility establish , and their utility procure . Even so in the worship of God , when rule be set down touch time , place , and ceremony of divine worship , accord to these ground , Christian liberty be not abridge , but confusion , schism , and disorder be prevent , decency and order be preserve . 1. Cor. 14. 33. God be not the author of confusion and unquietness , but of order and peace , in all the church of the saint . the contrary whereof must needs be , if in these thing indifferent , every man be permit to use his own will : for as many wit , as many will , as many head , as many diverse conceit . this meditation ( i hope ) be not unprope for this time ; wherein yet many be in the balance of deliberation unresolved whither to sway . some doubt of the act of the late assembly of our church hold at Perth , be determination of thing indifferent , or if they contain necessary point and ground of divine worship , whither faith will , they shall be obey or gain-stood . 1.
